title: chelonopsis odontochila subsp. bracteata
intro: Chelonopsis odontochila subsp. bracteata, also known as Mountain Mint, is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive minty aroma and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Mountain Mint include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Chelonopsis odontochila subsp. bracteata can be found in teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
